plan.summary: The dashboard's information architecture and data plumbing are genuinely good — every page pulls real pipeline artifacts and the demo story (70 sources → 2875 chunks → 41/14 drivers → 268M space → 120 scenarios → 5 archetypes) is all present in the payloads — but the UI is currently NOT safe to demo: clicking the CIB 'Influence' tab white-screens the entire app with the real data, any transient fetch failure blanks the SPA (no ErrorBoundary anywhere), and arrow-key navigation is dead on the Archetypes page, the single most important result screen. The second embarrassment tier is fabricated/stale hardcoded numbers sitting centimeters from correct live metrics (45 PDFs vs 70, 20 fake scenarios vs 6, 4 wrong personas vs 5, inverted AHP weights) — exactly what a methods-focused grader circles. Third, the combinatorial money shot undersells itself: 'null —' dash tiles, a missing driver-recipe heatmap (lost projection output, fixable by backfill), blobby oversized markers, and a flat green similarity square, while the supervisor's key traceability requirement hides a silent 65% dangling-evidence hole. The good news: almost every confirmed finding is a 1-to-20-line localized fix; the ranked list above is ~15-18 focused hours, front-loaded so the first eight items (crash-proofing, fake numbers, presentation mechanics, landscape money shot, traceability honesty) eliminate all P0 embarrassment risks — and nothing counts until the bundle is rebuilt, since FastAPI serves the prebuilt static assets.

confirmed.summary: OverviewPage crashes to a blank white screen if /api/overview fails: after a fetch error, loading=false and data=null, so `data.sources` throws a TypeError and there is no ErrorBoundary anywhere in the app to catch it.
confirmed.detail: useApi (web/frontend/src/hooks/useApi.js) sets error and leaves data=null on failure; OverviewPage only guards `if (loading)` (line 34) and never reads `error`. grep confirms no ErrorBoundary/componentDidCatch exists in main.jsx or any layout component, so the render exception unmounts the entire React tree. Same page also renders all-zeros silently if the backend returns the `{"unavailable": true}` sentinel (app.py line 90-94).
confirmed.fix: In OverviewPage.jsx, destructure `error` from useKbApi and add after the loading guard: `if (error || !data || data.unavailable) return <friendly retry panel>` (reuse the spinner container styling with a message + reload button). Ideally also wrap <Routes> in App.jsx with a tiny ErrorBoundary.
confirmed.demo_impact: If uvicorn isn't up yet, restarts, or the laptop's port is grabbed when the browser tab loads, the FIRST page of the live demo is a blank white screen with no explanation on the projector.
confirmed.verify_note: CONFIRMED with one severity caveat. (a) Code condition exists exactly as claimed: web/frontend/src/hooks/useApi.js:28-33 leaves data=null and sets loading=false on any axios rejection (5xx/network); web/frontend/src/pages/OverviewPage.jsx:30 destructures only {data, loading} — error is never read — line 34 guards only loading, and line 79 dereferences data.sources, throwing a TypeError on null. Grep over web/frontend/src confirms zero ErrorBoundary/componentDidCatch anywhere (main.jsx renders <App/> bare); React 19 unmounts the whole root on an uncaught render error, so the entire app — Overview is the '/' landing route — goes blank with no recovery except manual reload. The guard pattern the fix proposes already exists in-repo (ArchetypesPage.jsx:43 checks !data || data.unavailable), so this is an omission, not a design decision. (b) Trigger reality: I tried to refute via the real payloads and partially succeeded — /tmp/ep_overview.json is complete/healthy, and missing data files do NOT trigger the crash because app.py:90-94 converts FileNotFoundError to a 200 {"unavailable":true} sentinel (not an axios error). So no payload shape crashes it; the crash needs an availability event (backend restart/redeploy mid-session, Azure blip on the APP_PASSWORD-protected hosted demo, network drop) — realistic while iterating over the weekend with reviewers holding the tab open, and the blast radius is total. Severity is honestly P1 (event-triggered) rather than P0 (always-on), but the fix is ~3 lines. Secondary sub-claim (all-zeros on unavailable sentinel) is mechanically correct (MetricCard's useCountUp stays at 0 for undefined) but demo-mitigated: /tmp/ep_kbs.json shows the agriculture KB's views exclude '/', so Guarded in App.jsx renders NotForThisKb before OverviewPage can receive the sentinel — treat that part as low priority. Recommended fix as claimed: destructure error in OverviewPage, add `if (error || !data || data.unavailable)` retry panel after the loading guard; optional tiny ErrorBoundary around <Routes> in App.jsx (no page in the app reads error, so the boundary also covers the nine other pages with the same pattern, e.g. PipelinePage.jsx:631).

confirmed.summary: '2875 chunks' is displayed under the 'BOM Decomp.' node, but chunks are KB text chunks (kb_state.chunks in app.py line 132), not BOM output — while the correct BOM/trend numbers (drivers_by_origin: 24 bom / 17 trend) sit unused and Trend Scanning shows no metric at all.

confirmed.summary: Manifestation hover tooltips for the top grid rows are clipped to near-invisibility by the overflow-x-auto scroll container, so the primary way to read full manifestation text looks broken exactly where the presenter will hover first.
confirmed.detail: Tooltip is `absolute bottom-full` inside the cell (lines 48-55), and the table wrapper at line 125 is `overflow-x-auto`. Per CSS spec, overflow-x:auto forces computed overflow-y to auto, and content above a scroll container's top edge is clipped (block-start overflow is unreachable). Payload descriptions average 542 chars (max 721), which at max-w-xs/text-xs renders a ~250px-tall tooltip; row 1 has only ~40px of headroom above it inside the container, so hovering any first-row cell shows just the bottom 1-2 lines of the tooltip. Rows 2-3 are partially clipped too. Cells clamp descriptions to 2 lines (line-clamp-2, line 35), so hover is the only path to the full text.
confirmed.fix: Render the tooltip through createPortal to document.body with position:fixed coordinates from getBoundingClientRect (mirrors how it can then also escape horizontal clipping), or cheaper: pass rowIndex into ManifestationCell and flip placement to `top-full` for the first ~3 rows.
confirmed.demo_impact: The presenter hovering the first driver row on the projector gets a visibly amputated tooltip — reads as a rendering bug in the first 10 seconds on this page.

Real payload verified: /tmp/ep_morphbox.json (matches backend /api/morphbox in web/app.py:197, consumed by MorphBoxPage.jsx) has 14 drivers, 56 manifestations, ALL with 396-721 char descriptions (mean 542.6; row 1 = 459-531), so tall tooltips always trigger. (b) Empirically reproduced in headless Chrome with the exact DOM/CSS and real row-1 text: computed overflow-y on the wrapper is 'auto' (forced by overflow-x:auto per CSS spec), making it a scroll container whose block-start overflow is clipped and unreachable. Measured: tooltip height 432px, only 45px (~2 lines) visible above the wrapper top; screenshot shows just the last two lines floating over the header, label and description gone. The claim actually UNDERSTATES the bug: the abs-pos tooltip shrink-to-fits to the 208px cell containing block, so max-w-xs never applies and the tooltip is ~432px tall (not ~250px), meaning rows 1-3 and marginally row 4 are all clipped. Demo-visible on first hover of the primary reading affordance. Fix suggestion (portal, or top-full flip for early rows) is sound, though the flip must cover ~4 rows given the true tooltip height.

confirmed.verify_note: Verified against actual code. 'N/A'}` as a JSX child; the line-206 `panelMetadata &&` guard never helps since panelMeta is an always-truthy object literal. Same bug class in ForceNetwork3D.jsx line 77: marker size 8 + (influence/maxInf)*18 goes negative for the same two nodes so Plotly hides them on the Embeddings page.
confirmed.fix: Use the real domain and clamp: scaleLinear().domain([Math.min(...infValues), maxInf]).range([7, 24]).clamp(true) in ForceNetwork.jsx line 61, and the equivalent normalized formula in ForceNetwork3D.jsx line 77 (e.g. 8 + 18*(inf - minInf)/(maxInf - minInf)).

Three additional nodes fall below the intended 8px minimum (r=3.08, 4.31, 6.77), matching the claim's 3.1/4.3. confirmed.summary: 139 straight edges on 14 nodes form a hairball, and the 14 node-pairs with opposite-sign couplings in both directions draw a green and a red line on the exact same segment, so one polarity visually masks the other and direction is unreadable (no arrowheads).
confirmed.detail: Payload matrix has 139 non-zero cells (68% density): 74 at +1, 52 at -1, 12 at +2, 1 at -2. 59 pairs have both directions non-zero and 14 of those have opposite signs; links are plain <line> elements between node centers (lines 124-138), so A->B (+) and B->A (-) overlap pixel-perfectly — at stroke-opacity 0.33 they blend to a muddy color and the topmost DOM order wins. 126 of 139 edges are |1| at width 2.5/opacity 0.33, drowning the 13 strong (|2|) couplings that carry the story. The 'strongest inhibiting' relation the stats cite is one of these masked pairs' neighbors.
confirmed.fix: Add a strength filter defaulting to |value| >= 2 with a toggle for 'all couplings' (13 edges tell a clean story), replace <line> with slightly curved <path> per direction (quadratic offset perpendicular to the segment) and an SVG arrow marker; keep |1| edges at opacity ~0.12 when shown.
confirmed.demo_impact: The 22%-negative-couplings headline (a key analytic result) is literally invisible in the default view — the chart reads as uniform green mush on a projector.
confirmed.verify_note: CONFIRMED. Verified against ForceNetwork.jsx and the real payload /tmp/ep_cib.json. Data exact: 14 nodes, 139 non-zero cells (+1:74, -1:52, +2:12, -2:1), 59 bidirectional pairs, 14 opposite-sign, 126 |1| edges vs 13 |2|. Code exact: links are plain <line> elements between node centers (lines 124-138, tick 171-179), no arrow markers, no curvature, so A->B and B->A overlap pixel-perfectly; |1| edges at opacity 0.333/width 2.5. Projector-visible: ForceNetwork is the DEFAULT tab of CIBPage (useState('network'), line 234). The claim even understates it: the matrix's only -2 (the 'Strongest Inhibiting' relation NetworkStats cites) is itself an opposite-sign pair (-2/+2), and row-major link order appends the +2 after the -2, so the green line paints over the red at identical endpoints/width — the strongest inhibiting coupling renders green. Only nits: claimed 68% density is actually 76.4% off-diagonal, and 'neighbors' should be 'the masked pair itself'; neither refutes. Suggested fix (|value|>=2 default filter + curved per-direction paths + arrowheads) is sound.

confirmed.summary: Any fetch failure (backend restart, 500 on KB switch) crashes the page: after loading turns false, data stays null and line 244 dereferences data.panel_metadata with no !data guard and no ErrorBoundary anywhere in the app.
confirmed.detail: useApi.js sets error and loading=false but leaves data null on any axios error; CIBPage only checks `if (loading)` then immediately reads data.panel_metadata (line 244) -> TypeError. App.jsx mounts no ErrorBoundary, so the whole SPA unmounts. /api/cib re-fetches on every KB switch (useKbApi), and app.py's load('cib_state', kb) raises FileNotFoundError -> 500 for a KB without the artifact (the Guarded route mitigates only if /api/kbs loaded correctly).
confirmed.fix: In CIBPage add `const { data, loading, error } = useKbApi(...)` and `if (error || !data) return <Card>Could not load CIB data</Card>` before line 244; ideally also add a small ErrorBoundary around <Routes> in App.jsx as demo insurance.
confirmed.demo_impact: A momentary backend hiccup or an accidental KB-switch during the live demo turns the page into an unrecoverable white screen instead of a graceful message.
confirmed.verify_note: CONFIRMED (core claim), with one evidence sub-claim refuted. Verified against actual code: web/frontend/src/hooks/useApi.js lines 28-33 set error and loading=false on any axios failure but leave data=null; web/frontend/src/pages/CIBPage.jsx line 233 destructures only { data, loading } (error ignored), line 236 checks only `if (loading)`, then lines 244-247 dereference `data.panel_metadata?...` unguarded -> TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'panel_metadata') (reproduced via node simulation). grep confirms zero ErrorBoundary/componentDidCatch anywhere in web/frontend/src, so React 18 unmounts the entire root -> full white screen, recoverable only by manual reload. Every navigation to /cib is a fresh mount (data=null), so any backend restart, Azure cold start/502, or genuine 500 while clicking the CIB tab during the demo blanks the whole SPA silently. REFUTED sub-claim: the "500 on KB switch via load('cib_state') FileNotFoundError" path does not exist — web/app.py lines 90-94 register a global @app.exception_handler(FileNotFoundError) returning HTTP 200 {"unavailable": true}, and the live /api/kbs payload (/tmp/ep_kbs.json) shows agriculture's views exclude "/cib" (data/outputs_ag has only cib_state_zwicky.json), so the Guarded route shows NotForThisKb on KB switch. Note the sentinel is itself unhandled: if the Guard ever misses, data={unavailable:true} passes line 244 but crashes in QuadrantScatter/ForceNetwork (driver_ids undefined .map) — same white screen, different line. Nuance: mid-session KB-switch errors show stale previous-KB data rather than crashing (useApi never resets data), so the crash needs a first-fetch failure after page mount — a fault condition, but a realistic one for a live demo, and the suggested fix (destructure error, early-return a Card on error||!data, optional ErrorBoundary around Routes) is correct and cheap. Same unguarded pattern exists on every other page (none checks error or !data), so the fix generalizes.

confirmed.summary: No error/null guard: if the /api/bom fetch fails, data is null with loading false, and `data.total_nodes` throws a TypeError that white-screens the whole app (no ErrorBoundary anywhere in App.jsx).
confirmed.detail: useApi.js sets error and loading=false but leaves data null on any axios failure (server restart, wifi blip, laptop sleep/wake). BOMPage only checks `loading`, then dereferences data.total_nodes (line 33), data.total_drivers (34), data.tree (39). App.jsx has no ErrorBoundary, so the exception unmounts the entire React tree. Also does not handle the backend's `{"unavailable": true}` sentinel (app.py:94) the way ArchetypesPage.jsx:43 does.
confirmed.fix: After the loading check add `if (!data || data.unavailable) return <NotForThisKb />` (or a small error message using the `error` value from useApi, which BOMPage currently discards).
confirmed.demo_impact: One failed request while cycling pages with arrow keys crashes the entire dashboard to a blank white screen on the projector, requiring a full reload mid-presentation.
confirmed.verify_note: VERIFIED with qualifications — the core claim is real, one sub-claim is refuted. Checked: web/frontend/src/pages/BOMPage.jsx, hooks/useApi.js, context/KbContext.jsx, App.jsx, main.jsx, components/ui/MetricCard.jsx, components/viz/BOMTree.jsx, pages/ArchetypesPage.jsx, web/app.py, and real payloads /tmp/ep_bom.json + /tmp/ep_kbs.json. CONFIRMED (a): useApi.js:28-33 leaves data=null with loading=false on any axios failure; BOMPage.jsx:9 destructures only {data, loading} (discards error), checks only `loading` (line 11), then dereferences data.total_nodes (33), data.total_drivers (34), data.tree (39) — exact lines match. `grep -rn ErrorBoundary/componentDidCatch/getDerivedStateFromError` over src/ = zero hits; main.jsx adds none. So a failed FIRST fetch of /api/bom (page remounts fresh on each nav, so every visit starts data=null) throws TypeError in render and React unmounts the ENTIRE tree — full white screen until manual browser reload. Trigger is real for the demo: frontend is static-served by the same uvicorn (app.py:560/index.html) and dev mode goes through vite proxy, so any backend restart/hot-reload/transient failure at the moment someone clicks the BOM nav item kills the whole app, not just the page. Roughly half the codebase already guards this (ArchetypesPage:43, DriversPage `data || []`, PipelinePage `data?.`, EmbeddingsLabPage `!data?.nodes?.length`), so it's a genuine miss, not an intended convention. REFUTED sub-claim: the `{"unavailable": true}` sentinel (app.py:94, status 200) does NOT crash BOMPage — data is then a non-null object, MetricCard's count-up starts at 0 so `count.toLocaleString()` renders "0", and BOMTree.jsx:109 explicitly guards `!tree` and renders "No BOM tree data available"; moreover the sentinel is practically unreachable because Guarded in App.jsx:18-23 blocks /bom for KBs whose views lack bom_state (real /api/kbs payload: agriculture has no /bom view; spectrum's real /api/bom payload is complete: 74 nodes/27 drivers/tree dict). So no crash is possible from any real payload shape — severity is P1 (fault-triggered demo killer), not P0 (spontaneous). The suggested one-line fix (`if (!data || data.unavailable) return <NotForThisKb />` after the loading check) is correct and cheap; note the same unguarded pattern also exists in OverviewPage, CIBPage (line 245 `data.panel_metadata`), LandscapePage (line 75 `landscape.metadata`), ScenariosPage, StrategyPage and MorphBoxPage, so an app-level ErrorBoundary is the higher-leverage weekend fix than patching BOMPage alone.

confirmed.summary: Hover tooltips are clipped and effectively invisible: they are positioned `left-full` on a full-width row inside an `overflow-y-auto` container, which forces overflow-x:auto, so every hover just spawns a horizontal scrollbar instead of showing the description.

confirmed.demo_impact: Rich per-component descriptions (the ESMW receiver spec details) are unreachable during the demo; hovering visibly janks the layout with a flashing horizontal scrollbar on the projector.
confirmed.verify_note: CONFIRMED, could not refute. (1) Code: BOMTree.jsx line 78 tooltip is `absolute left-full top-0 ml-2` (minWidth 160) relative to a full-width row div; wrapper at line 118 is `overflow-y-auto max-h-[600px]` with p-4, and per CSS Overflow spec overflow-y:auto computes overflow-x to auto, so the tooltip lands outside the scrollport (only ~8px sliver inside the 16px right padding). Additionally — worse than claimed — all non-root rows (73/74 nodes) are inside a framer-motion wrapper with static `overflow-hidden` (line 96), which fully clips their tooltips with no scrollbar at all; only the root row exhibits the horizontal-scrollbar variant. (2) Data: /tmp/ep_bom.json has exactly 74 nodes and all 74 have non-empty descriptions (57-200 chars), matching the claim. (3) Demo-visible: BOMTree is rendered by BOMPage.jsx (line 39), routed at /bom in App.jsx (line 33); onMouseEnter on every row triggers the tooltip, so every hover on the BOM page hits this. The description tooltip feature is completely non-functional in the live demo. Suggested fix directions (below-row positioning or fixed/portal tooltip) are valid.

confirmed.summary: No legend for the driver highlighting: once the is_driver fix lands, 27 pulsing emerald dots and green names appear with zero on-screen explanation of what they mean.

confirmed.detail: The emerald encoding (lines 53-54, 60-61) is the only categorical color on the page and is never keyed. The page also relies on hover-only 'L{n}' level indicators (line 71-73, 10px, opacity-0 until hover) as the sole depth cue besides indentation.
confirmed.demo_impact: On a projector, unexplained green blinking dots read as an error indicator rather than the headline insight (which BOM components drive the foresight analysis).
confirmed.verify_note: Verified against BOMTree.jsx, BOMPage.jsx, web/app.py, /tmp/ep_bom.json, and data/outputs/bom_state.json. (a) Code condition exact: emerald pulse-dot at BOMTree.jsx:53-55, emerald-400 semibold driver names at 60-61, hover-only 10px L{n} level cue at 71-73 (opacity-0 until group-hover); pulse-dot keyframe real in index.css:104-110 so dots visibly pulse. No legend/key exists anywhere on the BOM page — BOMPage.jsx renders only two MetricCards (Total Nodes / Total Drivers) with no color key; emerald is the sole categorical encoding on the page, and other viz components (ForceNetwork.jsx:299) DO have legends, so this is an omission, not a design convention. (b) Payload matches: ep_bom.json has 74 nodes / total_drivers=27, all nodes carry is_driver (currently all false because bom_drivers ids have 0/27 overlap with bom_nodes ids — confirming the pending 'is_driver fix' the finding is gated on; app.py:185 checks nid in driver_ids). Once that fix lands, 27 of 74 nodes render pulsing emerald with zero on-screen explanation — projector-visible, not a style opinion. Suggested fix viable: BOMPage already has data.total_drivers to pass as a prop. Caveat: finding is contingent on the is_driver fix shipping; today zero dots render (the companion bug is 'Total Drivers 27' stat with no highlighted nodes).

confirmed.summary: Marker size formula `8 + (influence/maxInf)*18` goes negative for the 5 negative-influence drivers, and Plotly clamps non-positive sizes to 0, so 2 nodes are fully invisible and 2 more are ~2-4px specks with orphaned floating text labels.
confirmed.detail: Real payload /tmp/ep_cib3d.json: influence values include -8 ('Privacy-preserving edge AI standardization...'), -7 ('6G adoption of reconfigurable intelligent surfaces...'), -4, -3, -1; maxInf=13. Sizes: -8 -> -3.08, -7 -> -1.69, -4 -> 2.46, -3 -> 3.85. Verified in bundled plotly.min.js: the bubble-size fn is `return Uot(l)&&l>0?Math.max(l,a):0` — non-positive sizes render as size 0 (invisible). Their 'Isolated' gray labels float in space with no dot, looking like a rendering bug; the invisible nodes are also unclickable.
confirmed.fix: Min-max normalize instead of dividing by maxInf: compute `minInf = Math.min(...infValues)` and use `size = 8 + 18 * (n.influence - minInf) / ((maxInf - minInf) || 1)` at line 77 (and mirror for the selection halo at line 95, though that one stays positive with current data).
confirmed.demo_impact: If anyone arrows into this page, the gray cluster shows disembodied labels with missing dots — reads as 'the graph is broken' on a projector, and the size-encodes-influence story is wrong.
confirmed.verify_note: CONFIRMED end-to-end. (1) Code: ForceNetwork3D.jsx line 77 is exactly `8 + (n.influence / maxInf) * 18` with maxInf=Math.max(...infValues,1); no min normalization. (2) Data: /tmp/ep_cib3d.json (dumped today from the live /api/cib/3d route in web/app.py:368) has influences -8,-7,-4,-3,-1 with maxInf=13; recomputed sizes -3.08, -1.69, 2.46, 3.85, 6.62 — match the auditor's numbers exactly; all 5 nodes fall in the 'isolated' quadrant. (3) Plotly behavior: the quoted clamp `return Uot(l)&&l>0?Math.max(l,a):0` exists verbatim in the bundled plotly.min.js; context shows it is makeBubbleSizeFn (sizeref=1, sizemin=0, diameter mode) — non-positive size renders as 0. So the -8 and -7 nodes are invisible and the -4/-3 nodes are ~1.2/1.9px-radius specks. (4) Visibility: EmbeddingsLabPage.jsx fetches /api/cib/3d and renders this component; trace mode is 'markers+text', so gray labels for the invisible nodes float in space with no dot — projector-visible (page text even claims '14 technology drivers' while only 12 dots render). Halo at line 95 stays positive with current data (11.4), as the auditor said. Suggested min-max fix is correct. Not an intended design: formula assumes non-negative (absolute-CIB legacy) influence, but contrastive/net influence is signed.

confirmed.summary: Click-to-focus does the opposite of dimming: per-point marker opacity arrays are silently ignored by scatter3d, and the per-trace edge `isConnected` check passes for every trace, so selecting a node brightens ALL 139 edges from 0.35 to 0.7 and dims no nodes.
confirmed.detail: Two compounding defects. (1) Line 79 passes `opacity: qNodes.map(...)` but scatter3d marker.opacity is scalar-only — verified in bundled plotly.min.js: `opacity:$g({},$2.opacity,{arrayOk:!1})` in the scatter3d attribute block — so the 0.15 dim for non-selected nodes falls back to default 1. (2) Lines 128/139 set one opacity per whole tier trace via `filtered.some(e => connectedIds.has(e.source) && connectedIds.has(e.target))`; connectedIds includes all neighbors, and simulation on the real payload shows even the least-connected node (11 neighbors of 14 nodes, graph 76% dense) lights up all 4 edge traces at 0.7 — including a trace whose only edge is not incident to the selection at all.
confirmed.fix: Rebuild traces on selection: split nodes into a dimmed trace (scalar opacity 0.15) and a highlighted trace (0.9); split each edge tier into 'incident' (`e.source===selectedId || e.target===selectedId`, opacity 0.7) and 'other' (0.04) traces instead of the trace-level `some(...)` check.
confirmed.demo_impact: Clicking a node during the demo makes the hairball STRONGER instead of isolating the node's connections — the one interaction the page offers visibly misbehaves.
confirmed.verify_note: CONFIRMED both defects against real code and data. (1) ForceNetwork3D.jsx:79 passes an opacity array to scatter3d marker.opacity; the bundled plotly.min.js contains 'opacity:$g({},$2.opacity,{arrayOk:!1})' exactly once, located inside the scatter3d attribute block (verified via surrounding context: z data_array, surfaceaxis, projection x/y/z), so per-point dimming to 0.15 is silently dropped and nodes never dim. (2) Replicated the tier/polarity/isConnected logic (lines 109-143) in Python against the real payload /tmp/ep_cib3d.json (14 nodes, 139 edges, scores {-2,-1,1,2} => exactly 4 edge traces): for ALL 14 possible node selections, all 4 traces pass the some(connectedIds.has(src)&&connectedIds.has(tgt)) check and go 0.35->0.7; for 12/14 selections a bright trace's only edge is not even incident to the selection; the 0.04 dim branch is unreachable on this data. Page is live (EmbeddingsLabPage fetches /api/cib/3d and wires onNodeClick), so a demo viewer clicking any node sees the whole hairball brighten with no node dimming - the opposite of focus. Minor evidence quibbles only: least-connected node has 10 neighbors (not 11) and 76% density holds only for directed pairs (undirected is 88%); these do not weaken the finding. Suggested fix (incidence-split traces) is sound.

confirmed.summary: Every node select/deselect snaps the camera back to the hardcoded eye (2.0, 2.0, 1.2) because Plotly.react is given a fixed scene.camera with no uirevision, and deselecting restarts auto-rotation from angle 0 causing a second jump.
confirmed.detail: The effect at lines 149-213 re-runs Plotly.react on every selectedId change with `camera: { eye: { x: 2.0, y: 2.0, z: 1.2 } }` and no `uirevision`, so any camera position reached by auto-rotation or manual dragging is discarded on click. The rotation loop (lines 185-204) also resets `angle = 0` each effect run, so the first rotation frame jumps the eye to (~2.5, 0, 1.0) — including a visible snap right when the page mounts (initial eye is (2,2,1.2), first frame is (2.5,0.01,1.0)).
confirmed.fix: Add `uirevision: 'cib3d'` to the layout and `uirevision: 'cib3d'` inside scene (lines 153-169) so Plotly.react preserves the current camera; initialize `angle` from the current camera (`Math.atan2(eye.y, eye.x)`) instead of 0, or set the initial eye to (2.5, 0, 1.0) to match the rotation path.
confirmed.demo_impact: Each click during a live walkthrough yanks the viewpoint — looks glitchy on a projector and disorients the audience mid-explanation.
confirmed.verify_note: Verified against web/frontend/src/components/viz/ForceNetwork3D.jsx and the real payload /tmp/ep_cib3d.json. (a) Code condition exists exactly as claimed: the effect at lines 149-213 depends on selectedId and calls Plotly.react (line 171) with a hardcoded scene.camera.eye (2.0, 2.0, 1.2) at line 159 and no uirevision anywhere in the layout, so any camera state produced by the rotation loop's Plotly.relayout (line 192, which mutates gd.layout.scene.camera.eye) or by manual dragging is diffed and overwritten on every select/deselect. The rotation loop (185-204) re-initializes angle=0 each effect run with r=2.5, z=1.0, so the first frame jumps the eye from (2,2,1.2) [azimuth 45 deg, r about 2.83] to about (2.5, 0.01, 1.0) [azimuth about 0 deg] -- a visible jump on mount and a second jump on every deselect after the react snap-back. plotly_relayouting only fires on interactive drags, not programmatic relayout, so auto-rotation genuinely runs (about 14 deg/s) and moves the camera away from the hardcoded eye, making the snap-back visible after under a second. (b) Live-demo visible: EmbeddingsLabPage.jsx renders ForceNetwork3D (line 66) with selectedId as page state toggled by node clicks (handleNodeClick lines 18-20; ConnectionPanel onSelectNode toggles it too), and /tmp/ep_cib3d.json contains 14 nodes with the exact keys consumed (id/name/x/y/z/influence/dependence) plus 139 edges, so nodes render and are clickable with real data. The page also resizes the plot column on select, compounding the jolt. Claim, line numbers, mechanism, and suggested fix all check out; no refutation found.

confirmed.summary: All 139 edges of a 76%-dense 14-node digraph are always drawn (green/red lines at opacity 0.35), producing an unreadable rotating hairball in which the claimed proximity structure is invisible.
confirmed.detail: Payload: 139 edges over 14 nodes (max 182 directed pairs), scores Counter({1: 74, -1: 52, 2: 12, -2: 1}) — so 126 of 139 edges are weak |score|=1 noise, drawn at width 1.5. Nodes occupy a tight UMAP region (x 3.1-5.3, y -3.1..-1.1, z 2.2-4.7), so nearly every line crosses the whole cluster. The page copy promises 'closer nodes have stronger mutual influence' but the wireframe ball hides exactly that.
confirmed.fix: Default the edge display to |score| >= 2 only (13 edges — instantly readable) and/or drop the |score|=1 tier opacity to ~0.06; optionally add a small 'show weak links' toggle in EmbeddingsLabPage.jsx. Alternatively draw edges only when a node is selected.
confirmed.demo_impact: This is the page's centerpiece and the prime 'looks like ass' candidate: a spinning red/green ball of yarn communicates nothing on a projector.
confirmed.verify_note: Verified against real payload /tmp/ep_cib3d.json: 14 nodes, 139 edges (76.4% of 182 directed pairs), scores Counter({1:74, -1:52, 2:12, -2:1}), node coords in tight ~2-unit ranges (x 3.11-5.29, y -3.10..-1.14, z 2.22-4.67) — all exactly as claimed. Backend web/app.py:424-432 emits every nonzero matrix cell with no threshold; EmbeddingsLabPage.jsx:68 passes data.edges unfiltered; ForceNetwork3D.jsx EDGE_TIERS has min-1 tier (width 1.5) and line 139 renders all edge traces at opacity 0.35 when no node is selected, with auto-rotation on by default. Page copy (EmbeddingsLabPage.jsx:54) does promise 'closer nodes have stronger mutual influence'. So the default demo view is a rotating 139-edge wireframe where 126 weak |score|=1 edges dominate — projector-visible, not a style nit. Only mitigation is the selected-node state (unconnected traces dim to 0.04), which requires a click and doesn't fix the landing view. Minor claim imprecision (edges not literally 'always' at 0.35 — selection dims them) does not affect the verdict. Suggested fix (default |score|>=2 or weak-tier opacity ~0.06) is consistent with the existing tier design.

confirmed.summary: Edge polarity colors (promoting #10b981, inhibiting #ef4444) are the exact same hexes as the node quadrant colors (enabler #10b981, critical #ef4444), so red/green encode two unrelated meanings in one chart.
confirmed.detail: CIB_QUADRANT_COLORS in web/frontend/src/utils/colors.js:26-31 uses critical:'#ef4444', enabler:'#10b981'; ForceNetwork3D line 129 uses the identical colors for edge polarity. With the real payload, 7 of 14 nodes are 'critical' red — indistinguishable from inhibiting edges. There is also no legend entry for edge colors (showlegend:false), only the prose sentence on the page.
confirmed.fix: Give quadrant nodes a different hue family (e.g. blue #3b82f6 / violet #8b5cf6 / amber #f59e0b / zinc for critical/enabler/dependent/isolated) in colors.js, keeping green/red exclusively for edge polarity; or recolor edges to teal/orange. Add two dummy legend-only line traces ('promoting', 'inhibiting') so the polarity encoding is labeled.
confirmed.demo_impact: An examiner asking 'why is that node red?' gets an answer that contradicts what red means one pixel away — undermines credibility of the visualization.
confirmed.verify_note: Verified in code and real payload. (a) ForceNetwork3D.jsx:129 hardcodes edge polarity colors #10b981/#ef4444, identical to CIB_QUADRANT_COLORS enabler/critical in colors.js:26-31; edge traces are showlegend:false (line 141) while node quadrant traces populate the legend, so the only on-screen color key says red = 'Critical'. Page prose (EmbeddingsLabPage.jsx:54) says 'Edges colored by polarity' without mapping colors. (b) Replicated the component's median-split quadrant logic on the real /api/cib/3d payload (/tmp/ep_cib3d.json): medInf=4.5, medDep=4.0 -> exactly 7 of 14 nodes are 'critical' red, and 53 of 139 edges are inhibiting red (all in drawn width tiers). So the live chart shows red meaning two unrelated things, with the legend actively teaching the wrong meaning for red lines — projector-visible, not a style nitpick. One correction: the payload has ZERO 'enabler' nodes, so the green node/edge collision never manifests; only the red critical-node vs inhibiting-edge collision is real, which alone sustains the finding. Suggested fix (rehue quadrant palette or edges, add legend-only polarity traces) is appropriate.

confirmed.summary: LandscapePage dereferences `landscape.metadata` with no null/error guard, so a single failed /api/landscape fetch white-screens the flagship page (no ErrorBoundary exists anywhere in the app).
confirmed.detail: useApi (web/frontend/src/hooks/useApi.js:28-33) catches fetch errors by setting `error` and leaving `data` null with loading=false. LandscapePage only checks `loading` (line 65); line 75 then runs `landscape.metadata?...` on null -> TypeError. grep confirms no ErrorBoundary/componentDidCatch in App.jsx or main.jsx, so React 19 unmounts the whole root: blank white page. ArchetypesPage already does this right (`if (!data || data.unavailable ...)` at ArchetypesPage.jsx:43); LandscapePage skipped the guard. Trigger paths during a live demo: opening the browser before uvicorn finishes starting, backend restart mid-demo, or any 500 from the landscape endpoint.
confirmed.fix: In LandscapePage.jsx, destructure `error` from useApi (line 53) and add after the loading check (line 71): `if (error || !landscape) return <Card>Landscape unavailable — is the backend running? <button onClick={() => location.reload()}>Retry</button></Card>` mirroring ArchetypesPage.jsx:43. Optionally wrap <Routes> in a small ErrorBoundary component in App.jsx as a belt-and-braces catch for all pages. Then rebuild (`npm run build`).
confirmed.demo_impact: If the presenter opens the UI a second before FastAPI is ready, or the backend hiccups once, the centerpiece Scenario Landscape page becomes a blank white screen on the projector with no recovery except a manual reload.
confirmed.verify_note: CONFIRMED by reading actual code. (a) Exact as claimed: useApi.js:28-33 leaves data=null with loading=false on fetch error; LandscapePage.jsx:53 never destructures `error`, line 65 guards only `loading`, and line 75 `landscape.metadata?.n_scenarios` null-derefs (optional chain sits after .metadata) -> TypeError; grep of web/frontend/src finds no ErrorBoundary/componentDidCatch anywhere, main.jsx is bare createRoot with React 19.2, so the uncaught render error unmounts the root = white screen; ArchetypesPage.jsx:43 has the `!data || data.unavailable` guard LandscapePage lacks; Guarded/KbContext don't prevent the page from mounting. (b) Genuinely triggerable in the demo: backend restart mid-demo (SPA is served by the same FastAPI app in web/app.py, stays loaded in browser; next Landscape navigation or KB/method switch -> ERR_NETWORK -> crash, page stays white even after backend recovers until manual reload), Vite dev proxy 500s, or a real 500 (endpoint at web/app.py:485-491 catches only FileNotFoundError, so a corrupt/partially-written landscape_state*.json -> JSONDecodeError -> 500). Two corrections narrowing but not refuting the claim: (1) 'browser open before uvicorn starts' does NOT white-screen in the production setup — backend serves the SPA, so you get a browser connection error instead (only valid in Vite dev mode); (2) the missing-file case is already safe: the endpoint returns 200 with {"points": [], "similarity_matrix": [], "metadata": {}}, so landscape.metadata is {} and no crash occurs. Net: real, demo-visible crash on backend hiccup/500; suggested guard mirroring ArchetypesPage is the right fix.

confirmed.summary: `axisTitles` is a freshly-allocated array every render, which makes UMAPScatter's effect deps churn and Plotly.purge()+rebuild the main scatter on every parent re-render — live whenever the zwicky method is selected (its payload has non-null `axes`).
confirmed.detail: Line 90: `const axisTitles = axes ? [axes.pc1?.label, axes.pc2?.label] : undefined`. UMAPScatter.jsx's effect deps are `[points, onPointClick, axisTitles, colorBy]` (line 93) and its cleanup calls `Plotly.purge(ref.current)` (line 90). For the default cib/combinatorial methods `axes` is null in the real payloads (verified in /tmp/ep_landscape_combi.json and data/outputs/landscape_state.json), so axisTitles=undefined is stable and nothing happens. But data/outputs/landscape_state_zwicky.json carries non-null `axes` (PC1/PC2 labels), so with method=zwicky every LandscapePage state change — clicking a point (setSelectedPointId), toggling color mode — allocates a new axisTitles array, invalidates the effect, purges the chart and rebuilds it from scratch. No `uirevision` is set anywhere (grep confirms), so the presenter's zoom/pan and legend toggles are wiped, with a visible flash.
confirmed.fix: In LandscapePage.jsx replace line 90 with `const axisTitles = useMemo(() => (axes ? [axes.pc1?.label, axes.pc2?.label] : undefined), [axes])`. Additionally add `uirevision: 'keep'` to the layout object in UMAPScatter.jsx (line 70-76) so even legitimate data-driven re-renders preserve zoom state. Rebuild afterwards.
confirmed.demo_impact: In the morphological (zwicky) view, every click on a scenario point visibly flashes the chart and snaps the zoom back to full extent — exactly the interaction the presenter will do repeatedly on the projector.
confirmed.verify_note: CONFIRMED, with two minor caveats. Code condition (a) verified exactly: web/frontend/src/pages/LandscapePage.jsx:90 is `const axisTitles = axes ? [axes.pc1?.label, axes.pc2?.label] : undefined` (fresh array each render when axes non-null); web/frontend/src/components/viz/UMAPScatter.jsx:93 has effect deps `[points, onPointClick, axisTitles, colorBy]` and its cleanup (lines 88-91) calls removeAllListeners + Plotly.purge before Plotly.react rebuilds from scratch; grep for `uirevision` across web/frontend/src returns 0 hits, and DARK_LAYOUT (web/frontend/src/utils/plotly.js) has none. All other deps are stable across a selectedPointId re-render: `points` comes from useApi useState (same object reference until URL changes, verified in web/frontend/src/hooks/useApi.js), `onPointClick` is useCallback([]), and `colorBy` resolves to the stable string 'type' for zwicky (its points carry no `archetype`). So axisTitles is the sole churning dep. Data condition verified: web/app.py serves data/outputs/landscape_state_zwicky.json verbatim for kb=spectrum&method=zwicky, and that file has non-null axes with pc1.label/pc2.label (120 points, cx/cy present); landscape_state.json (cib) has no axes key and /tmp/ep_landscape_combi.json has axes:null, so those methods are unaffected exactly as claimed. Reachability (b) verified: /tmp/ep_kbs.json lists methods ["cib","combi","zwicky"] for the spectrum KB, so zwicky is one of three headline method buttons in the demo. Live trigger is the page's core interaction: clicking any scatter point fires setSelectedPointId -> parent re-render -> new axisTitles array -> purge + full rebuild, wiping drag-zoom (dragmode zoom still works with modebar hidden) and legend-toggle state with no uirevision to restore it -- projector-visible the moment the presenter zooms/filters and then clicks a point to open the detail card. Two overclaims that do not invalidate the finding: (1) 'toggling color mode' is not a real trigger on zwicky -- hasArchetypes is false there so the color toggle is not even rendered, and a colorBy change would be a legitimate dep change anyway; (2) the 'visible flash' is doubtful because the effect cleanup (purge) and next Plotly.react run synchronously in the same passive-effect flush, so the blank frame likely never paints -- the visible symptom is the zoom/pan/legend reset, not flicker. Suggested fix (useMemo on axisTitles + uirevision in layout) is correct and low-risk. Severity arguably P2 rather than P1 (requires zoom-or-legend interaction followed by a point click), but the defect is real with the real payload shapes.

confirmed.summary: For the flagship Combinatorial method, the three structure stat tiles (Dominant axis PC1, Effective dimensions, Silhouette vs. null) always render as em-dashes with a literal 'null —' subtitle and a false amber warning, because the combi payload's structure object only contains {lenses, floor}.
confirmed.detail: /tmp/ep_landscape_combi.json structure keys = ['lenses','floor'] — no pc1_share, effective_dim, best_silhouette, null, or has_usable_clusters (those fields only exist in landscape_state_zwicky.json). LandscapePage.jsx:153 gates on `isSampled && structure`, which is truthy for combi, so StructStat renders pct(undefined)='—', sub `null ${pct(undefined)}` = 'null —', and warn={!undefined}=true turns the silhouette tile amber. The intended fallback MetricCards (Clusters/Representatives, lines 163-167) are unreachable for combi.
confirmed.fix: Change the gate at line 153 to `isSampled && structure?.pc1_share != null` so combi falls through to the Clusters/Representatives MetricCards. In that fallback, derive the cluster count from the named archetypes in the points (5) rather than metadata.n_clusters (=9 in the combi payload), which would contradict the Archetypes page's '5 archetypes'. Also reword the sub from 'null 7%' to 'random baseline: 7%' for the zwicky path.
confirmed.demo_impact: The first click on 'Combinatorial' — the method with the named archetypes that IS the Monday story — shows a stats row that looks broken (three dashes, amber warning, the word 'null') on the projector before anything else.
confirmed.verify_note: CONFIRMED, tried to refute and could not. (a) Data condition is real and permanent, not a stale snapshot: both /tmp/ep_landscape_combi.json AND the live source file /Users/paulkeck/localdocs/lmu/dilab/data/outputs/landscape_state_combi.json have structure keys exactly ['lenses','floor']; the writer run_full.py:276-277 only ever sets structure.lenses + structure.floor for combi (structure.analyze_multi in src/pipeline/structure.py:297 returns lenses/floor), while pc1_share/effective_dim/best_silhouette/null/has_usable_clusters are only written by projection.py:192-200 and only present in landscape_state_zwicky.json (verified by loading both files). (b) Reachable in the live demo: /api/kbs lists spectrum methods ['cib','combi','zwicky'] so the Combinatorial toggle renders; web/app.py:485-490 serves landscape_state_combi.json verbatim for method=combi. LandscapePage.jsx:153 gates on `isSampled && structure`, truthy for combi, so the three StructStat tiles render: PC1 tile value pct(undefined)='—' with literal sub 'null —' (line 156); Effective dimensions '—'; Silhouette tile '—' in amber (warn=!undefined=true, line 161) — and its sub (line 160) is actually the literal string 'null undefined', slightly worse than the auditor claimed. The intended fallback Clusters/Representatives MetricCards (lines 163-167) are dead code for combi. Side-claims also check out: combi metadata.n_clusters=9 vs. /tmp/ep_archetypes.json's 5 archetypes, and points carry 5 named archetypes + 'Continuum'. This is projector-visible garbage in the stats row of the flagship method's page. Suggested fix direction (gate on structure?.pc1_share != null and derive cluster count from archetype labels) is sound; only nit: the 'null 7%' rewording applies to the zwicky path, cosmetic.

confirmed.summary: The Pairwise Similarity heatmap renders as a nearly uniform green square because zmin=0/zmax=1 is hardcoded while all real similarity values sit between 0.866 and 1.0.
confirmed.detail: CIB landscape payload (/tmp/ep_landscape.json): 6x6 matrix, off-diagonal min 0.8659 / max 0.9293, diagonal 1.0. With zmin:0, zmax:1 and the [#1e1b4b -> #3b82f6 -> #10b981] ramp, every cell maps to the top ~13% of the scale — the entire chart is one shade of green and communicates nothing. The three-stop indigo->blue->green ramp is also not perceptually monotonic in lightness. Colorbar uses the deprecated `titlefont` key (ignored by plotly.js v3).
confirmed.fix: Compute zmin/zmax from the off-diagonal data (e.g. zmin = floor(min offdiag, 2 decimals), zmax = 1), null out the diagonal (or set hoverongaps:false), and switch to a perceptual ramp such as 'Viridis' or a single-hue blues scale. Since it is only ever a 6x6 matrix (showHeatmap is CIB-only), also print the values in the cells via texttemplate '%{z:.2f}' at size >=12. Replace `titlefont` with `title: {text:'cosine similarity', font:{...}}`.
confirmed.demo_impact: This is a prime 'looks like ass' candidate: a big flat green square titled 'Pairwise Similarity' at the bottom of the Landscape page, visible whenever the CIB method is shown.
confirmed.verify_note: Verified against code and real payload. (a) Code condition exists exactly as claimed: SimilarityHeatmap.jsx lines 32-42 hardcode zmin:0/zmax:1 with a [#1e1b4b, #3b82f6, #10b981] colorscale and a deprecated colorbar `titlefont` key (plotly.js-dist-min ^3.6.0). (b) Real data matches: /tmp/ep_landscape.json (CIB) has a 6x6 similarity_matrix with off-diagonal 0.8659-0.9293, diagonal 1.0. Gating confirmed in LandscapePage.jsx (showHeatmap = !isSampled, isSampled = method !== 'cib'), so the wider-range combi matrix (0.0-0.7857) never reaches this component — no rescue path. Computed actual rendered colors: all off-diagonal cells fall in rgb(28,170,160)..rgb(22,177,146), diagonal rgb(16,185,129); CIE76 delta-E ~12.8 across the ENTIRE data range and typical cell-to-cell steps <4 (below/near JND), i.e. the chart renders as one flat teal-green square with unreadable internal structure — clearly projector-visible and genuinely hurts the demo (visually implies all 6 scenarios are identical). Minor correction to the auditor: the `titlefont` point is moot in itself since no colorbar title text is set at all (nothing visible is lost); the real gap there is the missing colorbar label. Core P1 finding stands; suggested fix (data-driven zmin, perceptual ramp, in-cell texttemplate for the 6x6 case, proper colorbar title) is appropriate.

confirmed.summary: The landscape scatter overplots badly: 120 points are drawn as 19-21px diamonds (base size 10 + consistency_score/10 with scores 91-114), so the size encoding spans a meaningless 2px while making every marker huge, and the circle/diamond symbol split is dead because is_fixed_point is false for all 120 combi points.
confirmed.detail: /tmp/ep_landscape_combi.json: consistency_score min 91.3 / max 114.0, is_fixed_point false for all 120 points, is_representative true for 9. Line 63 computes size = (10 + score/10) * (rep ? 1.8 : 1) -> 19.1-21.4px for normal points, ~35-38px for representatives, in a 500px-tall plot. Line 64 maps symbol to diamond for every point. The 1px white outline on 120 overlapping diamonds adds visual noise; clusters read as one smeared blob.
confirmed.fix: In UMAPScatter.jsx line 63, min-max normalize consistency_score across the payload and map to ~7-14px (or drop size encoding and use a fixed 9px), add marker opacity ~0.85, keep the 1.8x ring only for representatives, and use 'circle' for everything unless a payload actually mixes fixed points and samples. Add a one-line caption or legend entry explaining 'large ringed = archetype representative' (currently the encoding is explained nowhere).
confirmed.demo_impact: The scenario landscape scatter is the centerpiece slide of the demo; right now the 5 archetype clusters + continuum read as an overlapping blob of fat diamonds instead of visibly separated groups.
confirmed.verify_note: Verified against web/frontend/src/components/viz/UMAPScatter.jsx (lines 63-66) and the live payload /tmp/ep_landscape_combi.json (fed to UMAPScatter via LandscapePage.jsx:233, /api/landscape?method=combi). Code condition exists exactly as claimed: size = (10 + consistency_score/10) * (is_representative ? 1.8 : 1); payload has 120 points with scores 91.33-114.0 -> normal markers 19.1-21.4px (a meaningless 2.3px dynamic range) and 9 representatives at 34.4-38.3px in a 500px-tall plot; is_fixed_point is false for all 120, so line 64 renders every point as a diamond. Symbol split is dead per-view across all payloads: the CIB payload (ep_landscape.json, 6 points) is all fixed points (all circles), combi is all diamonds — no payload mixes symbols, and the 6-point CIB view shows the sizing formula was tuned for a tiny set and breaks at 120 points. Visibility quantified at rendered scale (~700x420px plot area): 39/120 points (33%) physically overlap a neighbor (21 pairs, min NN gap 7.6px vs 20px markers), and 14 of the 21 overlapping pairs are CROSS-archetype — colliding differently-colored cluster markers visibly blur cluster boundaries in the headline 'named archetypes + continuum' view; with a 9px marker only 4 points would touch. Representative encoding is explained nowhere on the plot (star tag only in post-click detail card, LandscapePage.jsx:280). One exaggeration: 'clusters read as one smeared blob' overstates it — median nearest-neighbor distance is 26.4px so most markers do not touch, and only 1 point's center is fully occluded by a representative; it reads as crowded with colliding edges, not a single smear. Core finding stands: dead size encoding inflating all markers ~2x plus cross-cluster overplot is real and projector-visible; suggested fix is appropriate.

confirmed.summary: Hover tooltips use '\n' as a line separator, which plotly.js does not render as a line break, so the tooltip shows title, archetype and score jammed into one very long line.
confirmed.detail:
Lines 56-57 build text as `${p.title} ... Score: ...` with hoverinfo:'text'. Plotly's SVG text renderer only honors '
' for line breaks in hover labels (' ' collapses to whitespace). With real titles like 'Audit-First Forensic Monitoring in a Fragmented Sharing Regime' plus 'Continuum' plus 'Score: 114.0' the hover balloon becomes a single ~80-char-wide line. DriverRecipeHeatmap.jsx:37 already does this correctly with '
'.
confirmed.fix:
Replace the two '\n' with '
' in the template string at UMAPScatter.jsx:56-57 (e.g. `${p.title}
${archetype}
Score: ...`).
confirmed.demo_impact: Hovering points live on the projector — the natural demo gesture on this chart — produces an ugly, hard-to-read one-line tooltip every time.
confirmed.verify_note:
CONFIRMED. (a) Code condition exists: web/frontend/src/components/viz/UMAPScatter.jsx:55-58 builds hover text with two ' ' separators and hoverinfo:'text'. (b) Library behavior verified directly in the shipped bundle: node_modules/plotly.js-dist-min/plotly.min.js defines NEWLINES=/( ?| )/g and its SVG text converter (convertToTspans path) does replace(NEWLINES,' ') before parsing, honoring only tags for line breaks — so title/archetype/score render as one long line, unwrapped, in the hover balloon. (c) Real payload triggers it: /tmp/ep_landscape_combi.json has 120 points incl. the exact cited example (title 'Audit-First Forensic Monitoring in a Fragmented Sharing Regime', 63 chars, max title 79 chars, archetype 'Continuum', score 114.0) → ~90-char single-line tooltip. (d) Viewer-visible: App.jsx:37 routes /landscape → LandscapePage which renders UMAPScatter (line 233); any hover in the demo shows it. DriverRecipeHeatmap.jsx:37 indeed uses
correctly, confirming the codebase convention. Suggested fix (' ' → '
') is correct and minimal.

confirmed.summary: There is no shared archetype color mapping: the 6-class palette (5 archetypes + Continuum) lives privately inside UMAPScatter, while ArchetypesPage renders all five archetype cards in identical blue, so the scatter's cluster colors cannot be visually connected to the named archetype cards.
confirmed.detail: UMAPScatter.jsx:13-15 defines CLUSTER_PALETTE + CONTINUUM_COLOR locally and assigns colors by alphabetical index of the archetype label. colors.js exports type/origin/confidence palettes but nothing for archetypes. ArchetypesPage.jsx uses a blue Sparkles icon (line 85) and a uniform blue size bar (line 100) for every archetype. /tmp/ep_archetypes.json has exactly 5 labeled archetypes matching the point labels in /tmp/ep_landscape_combi.json, so a deterministic shared mapping is trivial.
confirmed.fix: Move CLUSTER_PALETTE/CONTINUUM_COLOR into colors.js as e.g. `export function archetypeColor(label, allLabels)` using the same sorted-label indexing UMAPScatter already uses; import it in both UMAPScatter.jsx and ArchetypesPage.jsx, and give each archetype card a colored dot next to its title plus tint its size bar with the same hue.
confirmed.demo_impact: The core narrative move of the demo — 'this green cluster on the map is Federated Edge Assurance, here is its card' — has no color anchor; the audience cannot follow the cluster-to-archetype link across pages.
confirmed.verify_note: Verified all claims against real code and payloads. UMAPScatter.jsx:13-15 defines CLUSTER_PALETTE/CONTINUUM_COLOR privately and colors clusters by alphabetically-sorted label index (lines 34-36); colors.js exports six palettes but none for archetypes; ArchetypesPage.jsx renders every card with a blue Sparkles icon (line 85) and a uniform bg-blue-500/60 size bar (line 100) — no per-archetype color anywhere. Payloads match the claim: /tmp/ep_archetypes.json has exactly 5 labeled archetypes and /tmp/ep_landscape_combi.json's 120 points carry the identical 5 labels + 'Continuum' (40 pts), so a deterministic shared mapping is trivial. Demo-visible: LandscapePage defaults colorMode='archetype' and the combi payload triggers hasArchetypes, so the scatter shows 6 colored legend groups, while the routed, nav-linked /archetypes page shows the same 5 names all in identical blue — a projector-visible cross-view inconsistency, not a style nit. Minor caveat: archetype colors on the Landscape appear only after selecting the 'Combinatorial' method (default method 'cib' has no archetype labels and falls back to type coloring), but combi is the centerpiece of this branch. Suggested fix aligns with the existing sorted-label indexing. Finding stands; P1/ux is defensible (arguably P2 polish, but real).

confirmed.summary: /archetypes — the core-result page — is missing from PAGE_ORDER, so on that page arrow keys are dead, it is skipped when arrowing through the deck, and the progress dots vanish.
confirmed.detail: PAGE_ORDER (lines 4-15) lists 10 routes but not '/archetypes' (route exists in App.jsx:38 and SideNav.jsx:18). handleKey line 25-26 does `idx === -1 → return`, so ArrowLeft/ArrowRight do nothing on /archetypes; arrowing from /landscape jumps straight to /embeddings, skipping the named-archetypes result. Shell.jsx:31 hides the dot indicator when currentIndex < 0, so the dots disappear on exactly this page. Bonus: TopBar.jsx PAGE_TITLES (lines 4-15) also lacks '/archetypes', so the top bar shows 'radio spectrum monitoring systems / ' with an empty title there. Verified /api/archetypes has 5 named archetypes — this is the money page.
confirmed.fix: In usePresentationMode.js insert `'/archetypes',` after `'/landscape',` (line 13), matching the SideNav order (scenarios → landscape → archetypes → embeddings → strategy). In TopBar.jsx add `'/archetypes': 'Scenario Archetypes',` to PAGE_TITLES. No other changes needed — Shell derives totalPages/dots from PAGE_ORDER.
confirmed.demo_impact: During the live arrow-key walkthrough the presenter either never lands on the archetypes page, or clicks into it via the sidebar and finds the clicker/arrows dead and the progress dots gone — on the single most important result page of the grade presentation.
confirmed.verify_note: CONFIRMED. usePresentationMode.js PAGE_ORDER (lines 4-15) lists 10 routes and omits '/archetypes' ('/landscape' line 12 -> '/embeddings' line 13); handleKey lines 25-26 return on idx===-1 so arrow keys are dead on /archetypes and ArrowRight from /landscape skips straight to /embeddings. Shell.jsx line 31 gates the progress dots on currentIndex >= 0, so dots vanish on exactly this page; the hook is live (Shell.jsx:10). Route is real and enabled: App.jsx:38, SideNav.jsx:18, ArchetypesPage.jsx exists, and /tmp/ep_kbs.json shows the default 'spectrum' KB includes '/archetypes' in its views (not Guarded-off). /tmp/ep_archetypes.json contains 5 fully named archetypes with descriptions and distinguishing drivers — the core result page has real content. Bonus claim also true: TopBar.jsx PAGE_TITLES lacks '/archetypes' so the title renders empty there. Suggested fix is correct and sufficient (Shell derives totalPages/dots from PAGE_ORDER). Only nit: '/landscape' is line 12, not 13. Projector-visible on the money page; genuine P0 for the demo.

confirmed.summary: No ErrorBoundary anywhere and every page dereferences `data` unguarded after loading, so any failed API request (backend restart, port conflict, proxy hiccup) white-screens the entire app.
confirmed.detail: useApi.js:28-33 sets error and loading=false with data staying null on network error/5xx; no page consumes `error` (grep over pages: zero usages) and all render `data.x` right after `if (loading)` — e.g. OverviewPage.jsx:79 `data.sources` throws TypeError on null, and with no ErrorBoundary in main.jsx/App.jsx React unmounts the whole tree → blank white page requiring manual reload. Missing-file errors are masked by app.py:90-94 as 200 `{"unavailable": true}`; only ArchetypesPage.jsx:43 checks that flag (for spectrum all files exist, so the real trigger is a failed request, not a missing file).
confirmed.fix: Add a small class ErrorBoundary component (getDerivedStateFromError → dark-theme 'Something went wrong — press R to reload' card) and wrap `<Routes>` in App.jsx with it. Cheap extra hardening: in each page (or a shared `<DataGate data={data} loading={loading}>` wrapper) render the existing spinner/empty card when `!data` after loading instead of dereferencing null.
confirmed.demo_impact: One dropped request mid-demo (uvicorn restart, sleeping laptop, wifi blip) turns the projector completely white with no recovery hint; a reload also loses the current page's UI state.
confirmed.verify_note: Verified every element of the claim against the real code. useApi.js:28-33 confirmed (error path leaves data=null, loading=false); grep shows zero consumers of `error` in pages/components and zero ErrorBoundary/componentDidCatch/getDerivedStateFromError anywhere in src; main.jsx/App.jsx have no boundary. OverviewPage.jsx:79 (`data.sources`), BOMPage.jsx:33, CIBPage.jsx:245, LandscapePage.jsx:75 all dereference null data right after the `if (loading)` guard, so any failed request throws TypeError and React 18 unmounts the whole root -> white screen needing manual reload; the default route "/" is affected. app.py:90-94 confirmed masking FileNotFoundError as 200 {unavailable:true} with only ArchetypesPage.jsx:43 checking the flag, so the trigger is indeed a failed request (backend restart/port conflict/5xx via vite proxy or mid-session in built-static mode), not payload shape — real payloads (/tmp/ep_overview.json: sources=70, drivers_total=41, all keys present) render fine. One overstatement: not literally EVERY page is unguarded (DriversPage, ArchetypesPage, EmbeddingsLabPage, PipelinePage are null-safe), and severity is arguably P1 not P0 since the pure happy path can't trigger it — but the mechanism, evidence line numbers, and suggested fix are accurate, and a backend hiccup during the live demo would visibly white-screen the app.

confirmed.summary: The always-visible KB select keeps keyboard focus after use: arrow keys stop navigating pages and can silently switch the live demo to the thin 'agricultural harvesting robotics' KB.
confirmed.detail: kbs.length is 2 (verified by replaying /api/kbs logic against data/outputs*/), so the select always renders. After clicking it, focus stays on the SELECT; usePresentationMode.js:23 ignores keydown when target is SELECT, so arrows go dead — and on Windows/Linux Chrome Left/Right on a focused closed select changes the option, firing onChange and switching the KB. The agriculture KB exposes only ['/drivers','/landscape','/morphbox','/sources'], so 8 of 11 pages — including Overview and Archetypes — instantly become the NotForThisKb screen via the Guarded wrapper (App.jsx:18-23; verified it can never block spectrum pages since spectrum's views cover all routes). Arrow navigation also doesn't skip guarded-out views, so on the ag KB the presenter arrows through a wall of identical 'Not available for this knowledge base' pages.
confirmed.fix: In TopBar.jsx:27 change onChange to `(e) => { setKb(e.target.value); e.target.blur() }`. For extra demo safety, gate the switcher behind a flag (e.g. only render the select when localStorage.kbSwitch or ?kb= is present) and show the static 'R&S Horizon 35' brand label otherwise. Optionally make usePresentationMode skip PAGE_ORDER entries not in the active KB's views (read views from useKb()).
confirmed.demo_impact: One stray click on the top bar and the clicker/arrows stop working; one more arrow press can flip the whole dashboard to the agriculture proof-run, replacing the Overview/Archetypes pages on screen with 'Not available for this knowledge base' in front of the graders.
confirmed.verify_note: Verified against code and real data. (1) TopBar.jsx:24-27: select renders when kbs.length>1, onChange=setKb with no blur(); no focus mitigation anywhere in frontend src. (2) Replayed /api/kbs (web/app.py:456-482 with KB_DIRS/VIEW_REQUIREMENTS/FILE_ALIASES) against data/outputs/ and data/outputs_ag/: both KBs qualify (outputs_ag has landscape_state_zwicky.json, kb_state.json, functional_merge_state.json, morphbox_zwicky_state.json), agriculture views = exactly ['/drivers','/landscape','/morphbox','/sources'], matching /tmp/ep_kbs.json — so the select always renders on the demo box. (3) usePresentationMode.js:23 returns early for SELECT targets, and focus stays on a select after mouse use, so arrow paging goes dead on ALL platforms after touching the switcher; the silent KB-switch via Left/Right on a closed select is real but Windows/Linux-Chrome-specific (claim correctly scopes this; on macOS arrows open the picker instead). (4) App.jsx:18-23 Guarded confirms 8/11 routes (incl. Overview, Archetypes) become NotForThisKb on the ag KB, spectrum's views cover all 11 routes, and PAGE_ORDER is static so arrow nav doesn't skip guarded pages (minor imprecision: /archetypes isn't in PAGE_ORDER, so arrow-walking hits 7 dead pages of 10, not 8 of 11 — doesn't change the finding). Demo-visible because usePresentationMode/arrow-paging is the app's core presenting mechanic and the KB switcher is itself a feature meant to be demoed; recoverable only by clicking elsewhere, which is non-obvious mid-presentation. Suggested blur() fix is sound. P1 is defensible at least for the cross-platform dead-arrows + guarded-page wall; the silent-switch escalation needs Win/Linux.

confirmed.summary: No scroll reset on route change: after scrolling down on one page, the next arrow-key page opens mid-scroll with its hero/headline off-screen.
confirmed.detail: grep shows no window.scrollTo/ScrollRestoration anywhere in src/. Pages are taller than the viewport (Overview hero + metric cards + pipeline; Scenarios list; Drivers grid), routing is plain BrowserRouter, and Shell keeps the window scroll position across the AnimatePresence page transition — so navigating from a scrolled position lands the audience in the middle of the next page.
confirmed.fix: In Shell.jsx add `useEffect(() => { window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'instant' }) }, [location.pathname])` (location is already available on line 9).
confirmed.demo_impact: During the walkthrough the projector repeatedly shows the bottom half of a page after each slide advance; the presenter has to visibly scroll back up, breaking the flow on every transition.
confirmed.verify_note: CONFIRMED. Shell.jsx (web/frontend/src/components/layout/Shell.jsx) keys a motion.main on location.pathname inside AnimatePresence with no scroll handling; grep over src/ finds zero window.scrollTo/ScrollRestoration, main.jsx uses plain BrowserRouter, and index.css has no body scroll lock — the window is the scroll surface and its position persists across route changes. Arrow-key presentation mode is real (usePresentationMode navigates a 10-page PAGE_ORDER), and the real payload (/tmp/ep_drivers.json: 41 drivers; ep_overview.json) makes multiple adjacent pages in that order taller than the viewport (Drivers ~14 grid rows, Overview hero+cards+pipeline, Pipeline 692-line stage stack, CIB/Landscape/Strategy unconstrained), so scrolled-down ArrowRight demonstrably lands the audience mid-page with the next headline off-screen; browser clamping doesn't rescue tall→tall transitions. Suggested fix is valid (location already on line 9). Minor correction to the evidence: ScenariosPage is height-capped (h-[calc(100vh-44px)] with inner overflow-y-auto), so the Scenarios example is wrong and transitions INTO /scenarios self-heal — but ~8 other pages exhibit the bug, so the finding stands as P1 for the arrow-key demo flow.

polish.summary: ReactFlow's default preventScrolling=true swallows mouse-wheel events over the inline diagram, so page scrolling stalls whenever the cursor crosses the 220px flow area while scrolling through the overview.
polish.detail: zoomOnScroll/zoomOnPinch are disabled (lines 203-204) but preventScrolling is left at its xyflow default (true), so wheel input over the flow does nothing at all — no zoom, no page scroll.
polish.fix: Add `preventScrolling={false}` to the <ReactFlow> props in PipelineFlow.jsx so wheel events pass through to the page.
polish.demo_impact: Scrolling from the hero to the methodology cards visibly 'sticks' mid-page during the live walkthrough — reads as jank on the projector.

polish.summary: Clicking the 'Knowledge Base' node navigates to '/' (the overview page itself — there is no /sources route in App.jsx), and inline node clicks also bubble to the wrapper div that opens the expand modal, so the KB node click just pops the modal unexpectedly.
polish.detail: PATHS.kb='/' (line 30); App.jsx routes contain no '/sources' even though app.py's VIEW_REQUIREMENTS lists it. In OverviewPage.jsx lines 97-105 the flow is wrapped in a div with onClick=setPipelineExpanded(true); node clicks call navigate() but the event still bubbles, so for the self-route KB node the only visible effect is the modal opening.
polish.fix: Add e.stopPropagation() in PipelineNode's onClick handler (line 48) and point PATHS.kb at a real destination ('/pipeline', or the drivers page) — or make the KB node non-clickable inline.
polish.demo_impact: If the presenter clicks the first node of the pipeline ('here is our knowledge base…'), the UI responds with an unrelated modal instead of the expected drill-down.

polish.summary: The three methodology cards use raw emoji icons (🔲 ⚖️ 📊) while the entire rest of the dashboard uses tinted lucide icons — platform-dependent rendering that clashes with the dark glass aesthetic.
polish.detail: METHOD_CARDS (lines 11-27) define icon: '🔲'/'⚖️'/'📊' rendered as a bare 2xl span (line 118); MetricCard and PipelineNode both use lucide icons in colored 10x10 tiles.
polish.fix: Replace the emoji strings with lucide components (Grid3X3, Scale, BarChart3) rendered in the same `w-10 h-10 rounded-lg bg-<color>/10` tile treatment as MetricCard, tinted per method (blue/violet/emerald to echo PIPELINE_COLORS).
polish.demo_impact: Emoji render differently (and sometimes in color-clashing Apple style) on the presentation machine; the methodology row is the last thing on screen before switching pages, so it sets the polish impression.

polish.summary: Driver count silently jumps from 41 (Step 4 'Unified Drivers') to 14 (Step 5 'Drivers') with no stage or label explaining the top-14 selection.
polish.detail: Merge metricsFn (line 429) shows drivers_total=41; Morph metricsFn (line 443) shows cib_drivers=14. run_full.py Stage 3 ('select drivers + manifestations') performs the 41→14 selection, but neither the morph description nor methodology mentions it, so the number chain 41 → 14 → 56 manifestations looks inconsistent.
polish.fix: Rename the morph metric to 'Selected Drivers' with value '14 / 41' and add one clause to the morph methodology string (line 441), e.g. 'relevance-ranked selection of the top 14 drivers →' before the Zwicky step.
polish.demo_impact: A grader following the numbers down the timeline hits an unexplained 41→14 drop mid-narration; the user would have to improvise the explanation live.

polish.summary: MergeVisual and FunnelVisual carry stale fallback numbers (19/5/24 drivers, 106 fixed points, 20 seeds) that would render as real-looking data if /api/overview ever returns the {unavailable:true} sentinel.
polish.detail: Lines 104-106 default to bom=19/trend=5/total=24 (real: 24/17/41); FunnelVisual lines 267-268 default to 106 fixed points (real: 510) and 20 seeds (real: 6). web/app.py's FileNotFoundError handler (line 90) returns {"unavailable": true} with HTTP 200, so a missing artifact file yields loading=false with none of these keys — the page would then show the outdated numbers with full confidence instead of an error state.
polish.fix: Replace numeric fallbacks with '–' placeholders (or gate the visuals on `data?.drivers_total != null`), and optionally have PipelinePage render a small 'data unavailable' banner when `data?.unavailable` is set.
polish.demo_impact: Low probability on demo day if artifacts are in place, but if the wrong KB is selected or a file is missing, the page fabricates plausible-looking wrong numbers instead of failing visibly during rehearsal.

polish.summary: When a scenario overlay is selected, the chosen manifestation's highlight is very subtle (6% white background, 12%-alpha glow) — on a washed-out projector the scenario path barely stands out beyond the dimming of the other cells.
polish.detail: Highlight style (lines 13-27): background rgba(255,255,255,0.06), 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.2) border, boxShadow with `${borderColor}20`/`${borderColor}10` (12.5%/6% alpha). Verified the overlay data itself is correct — all 6 scenarios' 14 assumption ids match the grid — so the interaction works, it just reads faint. This overlay walk-through ('one manifestation per driver = a scenario') is the page's best live moment.
polish.fix: Strengthen the highlighted state: 2px solid ring in the scenario type color (available via selectedScenario.type and SCENARIO_TYPE_COLORS), background rgba(255,255,255,0.10), and consider dropping dimmed opacity from 0.3 to 0.2 for more separation.
polish.demo_impact: The core interactive beat of the page — tracing a scenario through the box — reads as 'everything got darker' instead of 'this path lights up' on a projector.

polish.summary: Every one of the 14 driver rows shows an identical purple 'TREND' badge plus 'medium' confidence text, adding a full column of zero-information visual noise.
polish.detail: Payload check: all 14 drivers have origin='trend' and confidence='medium' (the CIB panel selected 14 of 41 drivers, all trend-derived). OriginBadge/ConfidenceBadge render per row (lines 158-164), so the sticky column repeats the same badge 14 times.
polish.fix: If all drivers share one origin, hoist it into a single header note ('14 trend-derived drivers, medium confidence') and render per-row badges only when origins differ (drivers.some(d => d.origin !== drivers[0].origin)).
polish.demo_impact: Repetition wastes the narrow sticky column and invites the question why only trend drivers appear in the box.

polish.summary: Column headers 'Manifestation 1-4' imply an ordinal scale that doesn't exist in a Zwicky box, and the thead is not sticky, so headers vanish immediately when scrolling the ~1400px-tall 14-row grid.
polish.detail: Headers are generated as 'Manifestation {i+1}' (lines 132-139) though columns carry no meaning — each row's manifestations are independent alternatives. thead has no vertical stickiness, only the driver column is sticky horizontally (left). After one scroll wheel tick the audience sees unlabeled columns.
polish.fix: Make the header row sticky (`sticky top-0 z-20 glass-solid` on the th elements, works inside the page scroll if the wrapper doesn't clip vertically — else move headers out of the scroll container), and rename to neutral 'Alternative A-D' or drop the numbering for a single spanning label 'Manifestations (alternative future states)'.
polish.demo_impact: Minor, but during scrolling the grid loses its frame of reference and the numbering can prompt a 'is Manifestation 1 the baseline?' question.

polish.summary: 3D network selection highlighting is logically broken (opacity is decided per whole tier-trace via .some(), so selecting a node highlights all 74 edges of the +1 tier) and the rAF auto-rotation calls Plotly.relayout ~60x/s, hammering the CPU and never resuming after a drag.
polish.detail: Line 128: isConnected = filtered.some(e => connectedIds?.has(e.source) && connectedIds?.has(e.target)) sets ONE opacity for the entire tier/polarity trace — with 74 promoting |1| edges the condition is virtually always true, so 'dimming unrelated edges' does nothing; worse, connectedIds contains all endpoints of edges touching the selection, so an edge between two neighbors that never touches the selected node also passes the check. Lines 188-204: rotation loop issues a full Plotly.relayout every animation frame (WebGL re-render each frame) and rotatingRef is permanently false after any plotly_relayouting. Component is mounted on EmbeddingsLabPage (/embeddings), fed by /api/cib/3d (14 UMAP-positioned nodes, 139 edges) — the spatial layout encodes only |coupling| distances, so it is mostly wow-factor.
polish.fix: When selectedId is set, build edge segments per-edge (or two traces: connected vs rest) so opacity is per edge, filtering with e.source===selectedId||e.target===selectedId; throttle rotation to ~30fps with a time-based angle and resume it a few seconds after interaction ends. If demo time is short, consider defaulting /embeddings to the 2D network and framing 3D as an extra.
polish.demo_impact: If the Embeddings Lab is shown, clicking a node visibly fails to isolate its connections (everything stays lit), and the constant relayout loop can make the projector laptop's fans and frame rate noticeable.

polish.summary: Node quadrant colors reuse the exact edge-polarity hues on the same canvas: 'critical' nodes are #ef4444 (identical to inhibiting edges) and 'enabler' nodes #10b981 (identical to promoting edges), so red nodes read as 'negative drivers'.
polish.detail: CIB_QUADRANT_COLORS (colors.js lines 26-31) collides with ForceNetwork.jsx line 128 edge colors (positive '#10b981', negative '#ef4444'). In the force network the two most salient node classes and the two edge classes share hues while encoding unrelated dimensions (influence/dependence quadrant vs coupling sign); the combined legend (ForceNetwork.jsx lines 300-317) shows the same green swatch twice with two meanings ('Promoting' line and 'enabler' dot).
polish.fix: Give quadrants a distinct palette not containing red/green, e.g. critical '#a855f7' (violet), enabler '#38bdf8' (sky), dependent '#f59e0b', isolated '#71717a', in colors.js — QuadrantScatter, ForceNetwork, ForceNetwork3D and ConnectionPanel all pick it up automatically.
polish.demo_impact: On a projector the audience will misread red 'critical' hub nodes as bad/inhibiting actors; a one-line palette change removes the ambiguity across all CIB views.

polish.summary: EvidencePanel has no error/empty state: if the traceability fetch fails (traceData=null) or returns {'error': ...}, the panel slides in showing only the heading, and the SectionConnector dots render unconditionally as orphaned decorations.
polish.detail: ScenariosPage.jsx:49 catch() sets traceData=null -> '!loading && traceability' is false -> body is empty below the title with no message. If the API returns 200 {'error':'Scenario not found'} (app.py:330, e.g. any future combi wiring or kb mismatch), traceability is truthy but has no assessment/assumptions/source_chain, so the user sees just the heading plus the two floating connector glyphs (lines 88 and 117 render outside the section conditionals).
polish.fix:
Add a fallback branch: {!loading && (!traceability || traceability.error) &&

No traceability data available for this scenario.

}; move each inside the conditional of the section that follows it.
polish.demo_impact: Low probability on the happy path, but if the backend hiccups mid-demo the evidence panel — the key feature — opens visibly empty with stray dots and zero explanation.

polish.summary: The rank-6 scenario displays 'TOPSIS 0%' (real topsis_closeness = 0.0) and ranks 3-5 all show identical '28%', which on screen reads as broken scoring rather than TOPSIS's by-construction worst-of-set = 0.
polish.detail: Real payload: 0.5383, 0.4617, 0.2827, 0.2827, 0.2827, 0.0 — formatted with .toFixed(0)% in ScenarioList.jsx:101 and ScenarioDetail.jsx:55. '0%' next to a fully written-out scenario invites 'why is this scenario worthless?'; the three-way 28% tie makes the ranking column look copy-pasted.
polish.fix: Render the closeness as a 2-decimal score (0.54, 0.46, 0.28, ...) with a 'TOPSIS closeness' tooltip/caption instead of a percentage, or show '—' with title='TOPSIS anti-ideal (0 by definition)' when value === 0; one-line changes in both files.
polish.demo_impact: Not a crash, but '0%' on a ranked scenario is exactly the kind of number a grader circles; a labeled 0.00-1.00 closeness score defuses the question.

polish.summary: The Driver Assumptions strip puts all 14 assumption chips in a ~2,000px horizontal scroll row, so on a fixed projector view only ~5 of 14 driver assumptions are visible and the rest require awkward sideways scrolling.
polish.detail: Every real scenario has exactly 14 assumptions; chips are min-w-[140px]/max-w-[180px] in a flex overflow-x-auto row.
