There is no scroll affordance (thin scrollbar on dark bg), and the parsed detail text after the em-dash is dropped entirely, so the strip under-communicates the scenario recipe that distinguishes the 6 scenarios from each other.
polish.fix: Change the container to a wrapping grid (e.g. 'grid grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-2' or flex-wrap) so all 14 driver states are visible at once; optionally add title={parsed.detail} on the chip for hover context.
polish.demo_impact: The assumption recipe is what makes the scenarios visibly different from one another; hiding 9 of 14 behind a horizontal scrollbar weakens the differentiation story on the projector.

polish.summary: The Driver-recipe heatmap and PCA axis labels are silently absent for the Combinatorial method because landscape_state_combi.json was generated without axes/parcoords (the zwicky artifact has both), so the weakest method shows the richest visuals.
polish.detail: ep_landscape_combi.json top-level keys: points/similarity_matrix/scenario_ids/metadata/structure — no 'axes', no 'parcoords', no cx/cy on points. landscape_state_zwicky.json has all of them (pc1_share 0.115, 120 parcoords rows, PC1 axis label). The page degrades gracefully (falls back to 'UMAP Dimension 1/2' axis titles and hides the Driver-recipe section via hasParcoords), but the demo's headline method loses its most explanatory chart (continuum as color bands) while the secondary zwicky toggle shows it.
polish.fix: Data-side, not frontend: re-run the projection/parcoords step for the combi landscape (the run_full wiring TODO) so landscape_state_combi.json gains axes/structure-verdict/parcoords like the zwicky artifact; no code change needed, but do it before Monday or drop the Driver-recipe claim from the talk track.
polish.demo_impact: If the presenter narrates 'rows sorted by PC1 show the continuum as color bands', that section simply is not on screen for the Combinatorial method — only for Functional/CCA.

polish.summary: If either API call fails (backend restart, network blip), loading becomes false with landscape=null and line 75 `landscape.metadata` throws, blanking the page — useApi's error value is never consumed and the app has no error boundary.
polish.detail: useApi sets error and loading=false on axios failure while data stays null; LandscapePage only guards `loading` (line 65) and then dereferences `landscape.metadata` / `landscape.points` unguarded. App.jsx mounts no ErrorBoundary, so the TypeError unmounts the whole route to a blank screen. Endpoint-side FileNotFound is safe (returns {points:[],...}), so this only fires on transport errors — but a mid-demo uvicorn reload or WiFi hiccup is exactly the failure mode you cannot afford live.
polish.fix:
In LandscapePage.jsx: destructure `error` from both useApi calls and render a small retry/fallback card when `!landscape` after loading (e.g. `if (!landscape) return

Could not load landscape — retry.

`), or minimally use `landscape?.metadata` / `landscape?.points` everywhere.
polish.demo_impact: One transient fetch failure during the live demo turns the entire Landscape page into a permanent blank screen until a manual reload.

polish.summary: The page defaults to the 6-point 'CIB Fixed-Point' view (plus the flat similarity heatmap), so the 120-scenario archetype money shot is hidden behind a toggle click, and every method switch replaces the whole page with a full-screen spinner (scroll/state jank).
polish.detail: useState('cib') at line 41; /api/kbs lists methods in order [cib, combi, zwicky] so activeMethod resolves to 'cib' on load — first impression is a sparse 6-diamond scatter with no headline and the uniform-green heatmap. The `if (loading) return spinner` at line 65 also fires on every method toggle because both useApi hooks refetch, unmounting all charts and resetting scroll to a centered spinner for the fetch duration.
polish.fix: Default the local method state to the richest available method (`useState('combi')`, falling back via the existing `activeMethod` guard for KBs without combi), and/or keep the page shell mounted during refetch (per-section skeletons instead of the full-page spinner) so toggling methods during the demo does not blank the screen.
polish.demo_impact: The audience's first view of the 'Scenario Landscape' undersells the result, and each live method toggle causes a visible white-flash/spinner jump on the projector.

polish.summary: The 'Attractors 0 — CIB fixed points' tile shows a zero on the flagship page while the Overview page reports 510 fixed points (ep_overview total_fixed_points: 510) under the same 'CIB fixed points' vocabulary — an inconsistency that invites an awkward live question.
polish.detail: archetypes payload: n_fixed_points=0 and every archetype has contains_attractor=false (so the attractor badge is dead code this run); overview payload: total_fixed_points=510 from the MC consistency stage. These count different things (fixed points among the 120 sampled combi configs vs. MC attractors of the CIB system) but the UI labels both 'CIB fixed points'.
polish.fix: Render the tile only when `data.n_fixed_points > 0`, or change the sub-label to something self-disambiguating like 'CIB attractors inside the 120 sampled configs' so it can't be read against the Overview's 510.
polish.demo_impact: A grader cross-referencing pages sees 0 vs 510 'fixed points' and asks which number is wrong; a zero-value tile on the core result page also visually reads as 'nothing found'.

polish.summary: Heatmap hover tooltips show raw 12-char hex scenario ids ('f8b26a8b478a') instead of scenario titles, although LandscapePage already holds the scenarios list with titles.
polish.detail: Line 37 interpolates `r.scenario_id` into the hover text; parcoords rows carry only scenario_id/type/pc1/values, but the parent LandscapePage fetches /api/scenarios with id→title (it already builds `scenarioTitles` for the similarity heatmap at lines 95-98).
polish.fix: Pass a `titleById` map prop from LandscapePage (reuse the existing scenarios lookup) and render `titleById[r.scenario_id] || r.scenario_id` in the hover template.
polish.demo_impact: Hovering during the demo surfaces meaningless hashes on the projector instead of scenario names, undercutting the traceability story.

polish.summary: 'Core silhouette' displays the raw 4-decimal value 0.3799 instead of the headline 0.38, and the continuum note at the bottom repeats the header paragraph almost verbatim.
polish.detail: Stat value is `data.hdbscan_silhouette ?? '—'` → renders '0.3799'; header (lines 62-66) and the continuum note (payload: 'archetypes describe only the dense core, not the whole field') say the same thing twice on one screen.
polish.fix: Format with `data.hdbscan_silhouette?.toFixed(2)` (guarding null), and shorten the header paragraph to one sentence so the honesty message lands once, prominently, via the bottom note.
polish.demo_impact: Minor, but 0.38 is the number the presenter will say out loud — the screen should match the spoken headline.

polish.summary: `for (const s of scenarios)` crashes with 'scenarios is not iterable' (white page, no ErrorBoundary anywhere in the app) when /api/scenarios returns the backend's `{"unavailable": true}` sentinel object instead of an array.
polish.detail: app.py's FileNotFoundError handler (lines 90-95) returns `{"unavailable": true}` with HTTP 200 when final_analysis.json is missing for the selected KB. The `if (!scenarios) return {}` guard passes for a truthy object, then for...of throws. Currently masked only because the Guarded route (App.jsx) greys out /strategy for the agriculture KB — but VIEW_REQUIREMENTS for /strategy checks only strategic_framing, not final_analysis, so any KB with framing but no final_analysis white-screens the page; the page also silently ignores the useApi `error` value.
polish.fix: Change line 35-36 guard to `if (!Array.isArray(scenarios)) return {}` and add the same Array.isArray guard before the 'Scenario Rankings' section (line 321, `Array.isArray(scenarios) && scenarios.length > 0`); optionally render a small 'strategy data unavailable' notice when `!loading && !hasFraming`.
polish.demo_impact: Low probability on the spectrum KB, but if the KB switcher is demonstrated (the domain-agnostic pitch) and artifacts are ever regenerated inconsistently, the failure mode is a full white screen mid-demo.

polish.summary: The horizon pill on No-Regret Moves renders the raw enum value 'short_term' with an underscore instead of human-readable text.
polish.detail: Payload horizons are 'immediate' and 'short_term'; line 183 interpolates `{horizon}` verbatim, so three of the five move cards show a 'short_term' pill on the projector.
polish.fix: Render `{horizon.replace(/_/g, ' ')}` (or a small label map {immediate: 'Immediate', short_term: 'Short term'}) at line 183.
polish.demo_impact: Small but visible raw-enum leakage on a hero demo section; a 10-second fix.

polish.summary: The High/Low scenario chips in Critical Uncertainties use 10px text with 38-char truncation and no hover title, leaving near-illegible, ambiguous chips on a projector (e.g. two different chips both ending '...in a Patchwor…').
polish.detail: Lines 136 and 146 render `text-[10px]` chips truncated at 38 chars + ellipsis; real titles like 'Forensic Spectrum States in a Patchwork Sharing Regime' and 'Receiver-Led Enforcement in a Patchwork Sharing Regime' get cut mid-word, and there is no `title` attribute so the full name is unrecoverable on hover. Section micro-labels ('High →'/'Low →', 'Immediate Actions') are also 10px.
polish.fix: Bump chips to `text-xs`, add `title={s}` for hover recovery, and shorten via a first-N-words heuristic or map to the scenario rank number (#2, #3) with the TypeBadge color instead of truncated prose; for the demo, projector-critical micro-labels should be 11-12px minimum.
polish.demo_impact: The '3 critical uncertainties' cards are a named demo beat; from the back of the room the scenario chips are unreadable dots of color.

polish.summary: A non-array /api/drivers response (the {'unavailable': true} FileNotFoundError sentinel from app.py) passes the `data || []` guard and crashes the page in useMemo with 'drivers is not iterable' — a white screen instead of the intended graceful fallback.
polish.detail: web/app.py lines 90-94 return JSONResponse({'unavailable': True}, 200) whenever a needed artifact file is missing ('this is the safety net'). That object is truthy, so line 20 `const drivers = data || []` keeps it; line 24 `for (const d of drivers)` then throws TypeError. ArchetypesPage.jsx:43 guards `data.unavailable` explicitly; DriversPage does not. Currently masked by App.jsx's Guarded views check, but the safety net itself is broken for this page (e.g. if merge_state.json is renamed/regenerated mid-serve, or Guarded renders before /api/kbs resolves).
polish.fix: Line 20: `const drivers = Array.isArray(data) ? data : []`, optionally rendering the NotForThisKb component when `data?.unavailable`.
polish.demo_impact: Low likelihood Monday (spectrum merge_state exists), but if artifacts get regenerated by a last-minute pipeline run while the server is up, the fallback path white-screens the app instead of degrading gracefully.

polish.summary: The 'Merge Reasoning' expanded block is dead on the real data — merge_reasoning is an empty string for all 41 drivers — so clicking any card reveals only the unclamped description and a chunk count.
polish.detail: Verified: 0 of 41 items in /tmp/ep_drivers.json (and 0 of 9 in the agriculture functional_merge_state) have non-empty merge_reasoning, so the conditional at line 134 never renders. The expand interaction (chevron, accent brightening, animation) therefore over-promises: the payoff is two extra lines of text and 'N source chunks'.
polish.fix: Either populate merge_reasoning in the merge_state artifact during the pipeline run, or replace the block with fields that DO exist — confidence (ConfidenceBadge already exists in ui/Badge.jsx) and axis_role — so expansion always shows something.
polish.demo_impact: If the user clicks a card live to show 'merge reasoning traceability' (a claimed feature), nothing appears; better to know now than on the projector.

polish.summary: Expanding one card stretches every sibling card in the same grid row (h-full + default grid stretch), leaving large empty glass panels next to the expanded card, and descriptions are ~600-960 chars so the row gets very tall.
polish.detail: Line 76 grid has no items-start; line 87 Card has className 'h-full', so when one card expands (~800-char description un-clamped + footer), its two xl:grid-cols-3 row neighbors balloon to the same height with dead space. Verified description lengths in payload: min 571, max 959 chars.
polish.fix: Add `items-start` to the grid container class at line 76 (or `self-start` on the motion.div wrappers) and drop `h-full` from the Card at line 87 so only the clicked card grows; the framer-motion `layout` prop already animates the reflow.
polish.demo_impact: On a projector the half-empty neighbor cards look broken/unfinished when the user clicks a driver to show its description.

polish.summary: 'Total Drivers: 27' contradicts driver counts elsewhere in the app (Overview shows drivers_total=41 with only 24 of origin 'bom'; Drivers page shows the 41 unified drivers), with no hint these are different populations.
polish.detail: /tmp/ep_overview.json: drivers_total=41, drivers_by_origin={bom:24, trend:17}. /tmp/ep_bom.json: total_drivers=27 (pre-merge BOM driver candidates from bom_state.json). Same word 'drivers', three different numbers across adjacent nav pages.
polish.fix: Relabel the MetricCard to 'BOM Driver Candidates' (or 'Drivers extracted from BOM, pre-merge') in BOMPage.jsx line 34 so the 27-vs-41-vs-24 mismatch is self-explanatory.
polish.demo_impact: A grader flipping between pages sees inconsistent driver counts and may question the pipeline's traceability — the project's explicitly stated quality bar.

polish.summary: Descriptions are hard-cut at 200 chars server-side with no ellipsis, and the frontend's `length > 200` ellipsis check (BOMTree.jsx:81) can therefore never fire, so tooltips end abruptly mid-word.
polish.detail: app.py:186 `n.get("description", "")[:200]`; e.g. the root node's 316-char description arrives as '...panorama scan at 2.6 THz/s, 75ns min' — truncated mid-spec. BOMTree.jsx lines 81-83 only append '...' when length > 200, which is impossible after the server slice.
polish.fix: In app.py append an ellipsis when truncating: `(desc[:200] + '…') if len(desc) > 200 else desc` — or send the full description and let the existing frontend logic truncate.
polish.demo_impact: Tooltips (once un-clipped) look broken/cut-off when shown on screen; minor but visible.

polish.summary: Expandable rows use `cursor-default`, giving no affordance that clicking toggles children; 44 of 74 nodes (all level-3 leaves) are hidden behind collapsed level-2 rows by default.
polish.fix: Make the cursor conditional on hasChildren: `className={...} ${hasChildren ? 'cursor-pointer' : 'cursor-default'}` on the row div (line 32). Optionally also add an 'expand all' control since default expansion (level <= 1) hides the entire leaf layer.
polish.detail: Payload level histogram: {0:1, 1:10, 2:19, 3:44}; TreeNode's initial expanded state is `level <= 1`, so all 19 L2 rows start collapsed and the chevron button is the only visual hint.
polish.demo_impact: During a quick live walkthrough the deepest, most concrete components (ADC, DDC, FFT unit — the actual driver sources) stay invisible unless the presenter knows to click through 19 collapsed rows.

polish.summary: Hover tooltips use \n as line separator, which Plotly renders as a space, so the tooltip is one long unbroken line like '5G security compliance and assurance mandates Influence: -4 Dependence: -5'.
polish.detail:
Line 72: hovertext: `${n.name}\nInfluence: ${n.influence}\nDependence: ${n.dependence}`. Plotly hover labels only honor
for line breaks; literal newlines are collapsed to whitespace, so with the payload's ~60-char driver names the tooltip becomes a very wide single-line label.
polish.fix:
Replace \n with
in the hovertext template at line 72.
polish.demo_impact: Hovering during the demo shows an awkward full-width one-line tooltip instead of a tidy 3-line card.

polish.summary: Node labels render at 9px zinc-400 and are truncated to 24 characters, which is unreadable on a projector and makes several drivers near-identical ('International shift towa...' vs 'International spectrum h...').
polish.detail: textfont size 9 (line 75) with shortName(name, 24) (lines 18-20, 70). The payload's 14 driver names share long common prefixes ('Shift to...', 'International...', '5G...'), so at 24 chars multiple labels look alike, all in low-contrast #a1a1aa floating over a dark background inside a rotating 3D scene.
polish.fix: Raise textfont size to 11-12 and color to #d4d4d8, extend truncation to ~34 chars, and consider labeling only the top-N influence nodes (e.g. influence >= median) with the rest label-on-hover to reduce clutter.
polish.demo_impact: From the back of the room the labels are gray fuzz; the audience cannot tell which driver is which.

polish.summary: '/embeddings' sits in PAGE_ORDER between /landscape and /strategy, so the presenter is forced through this experimental page when arrow-keying to the Strategy finale; its copy also hardcodes '14 technology drivers' and the 'Embeddings Lab' name misdescribes the content (UMAP of CIB cross-impact scores, not embeddings).
polish.detail: PAGE_ORDER lists '/embeddings' at index 8, directly before '/strategy' — it is not merely 'reachable', it is ON the arrow-key path (note /archetypes is absent from PAGE_ORDER entirely). EmbeddingsLabPage.jsx line 54 hardcodes '14 technology drivers' (currently matches data.nodes.length=14, but silently lies after any re-run) and claims a '3D UMAP projection' of embeddings while app.py:368-410 actually runs UMAP on a 1/(1+|cross-impact|) distance matrix. Failure modes are otherwise graceful: missing cib_state returns empty nodes -> 'No CIB data available'; agriculture KB shows NotForThisKb.
polish.fix: Either remove '/embeddings' from PAGE_ORDER in usePresentationMode.js (safest for Monday — it stays reachable via the side nav) or, if kept, replace the hardcoded '14' with `data.nodes.length` in EmbeddingsLabPage.jsx line 54 and retitle the copy to 'CIB influence topology (UMAP of cross-impact distances)'.
polish.demo_impact: During the live demo the user WILL land on this page when advancing from Landscape to Strategy; with the findings above unfixed, the weakest visualization in the app appears at the climax of the presentation.

polish.summary: Served bundle is currently FRESH (verified byte-identical hashes with a clean rebuild), but FastAPI serves this prebuilt web/static bundle, so any frontend edit before Monday will silently not appear without an explicit rebuild.
polish.detail: Staleness check result: newest src file (pages/LandscapePage.jsx) mtime Jul 8 19:23:39; deployed bundle built Jul 8 19:24:05 — 26 s later. A throwaway production build (vite build --outDir /tmp/ui_audit_build) reproduced the exact same content hashes (index-BQOTmYu5.js, index-CVefrWud.css), proving deployed == current source. vite.config.js is correctly wired: outDir '../static' matches app.py STATIC_DIR (app.py:35, mount at app.py:560), base '/static/' matches the mount, dev proxy localhost:8000 matches uvicorn's default port. Build itself is clean (1.6 s, no errors).
polish.fix: Add to the demo runbook: after ANY change under web/frontend/src, run `cd web/frontend && npm run build` (outputs straight into web/static via vite.config outDir) and confirm the asset hash in web/static/assets changed. Do this once more as the final pre-demo step Sunday night.
polish.demo_impact: None today, but if any of the other audit findings get fixed without rebuilding, Monday's projector will show the old code and the fixes will appear to have vanished.

polish.summary: `npm run lint` fails with 10 errors, but all are verified runtime-benign (unused vars, init-setState-in-effect patterns, react-refresh export rules); the one flagged hook-dependency warning in UMAPScatter (missing deps X/Y) is a false alarm.
polish.detail: eslint src --max-warnings=0: 10 errors / 9 warnings. Triaged: no-unused-vars in ConnectionPanel/ForceNetwork/SimilarityHeatmap/CIBPage/PipelinePage = dead code only; react-hooks/set-state-in-effect in useApi.js:12 and ScenariosPage.jsx:22 are standard fetch/select-first-item initialization, no cascading-render risk in practice; react-refresh errors in KbContext.jsx are dev-only; the UMAPScatter.jsx:93 missing-deps warning on X/Y is safe because hasPca derives solely from `points`, which is already in the dep array. The ref-cleanup warnings on Plotly purge patterns are the usual benign idiom. Nothing here causes stale renders on the real payloads.
polish.fix: No pre-demo action needed. Post-demo cleanup: delete the unused identifiers and add `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` (or the extracted-ref pattern) where the Plotly-purge idiom is intentional, so `npm run lint` can gate CI again.
polish.demo_impact: None for the live demo; only blocks a clean lint gate.

polish.summary: Production build emits one 5.3 MB JS chunk (1.6 MB gzip) because Plotly, @xyflow, framer-motion and d3 are all bundled into a single entry — Vite's chunk-size warning fires.
polish.detail: vite build: 'assets/index-BQOTmYu5.js 5,324.52 kB | gzip: 1,609.95 kB' plus '(!) Some chunks are larger than 500 kB after minification'. Irrelevant when served from localhost during the demo (loads instantly), only matters if the app is ever served over a network or deployed.
polish.fix: Post-demo: lazy-load heavy pages with React.lazy/dynamic import (Plotly-based pages especially), or set build.rolldownOptions.output.codeSplitting in vite.config.js. Not worth touching before Monday.
polish.demo_impact: None on localhost; a few seconds of white screen only if it were ever demoed over Wi-Fi from another machine.

polish.summary: The CIB impact heatmap's diverging RdBu scale is correctly centered at 0 but hardcodes zmin/zmax to ±3 while the real matrix range is −2..+2, wasting a third of the color range and washing out contrast; there is also no shared diverging/sequential scale, so each heatmap invents its own ramp.
polish.detail: /tmp/ep_cib.json matrix range is exactly -2..2 (std_matrix 0..0.89), but CIBPage.jsx:113 sets zmin:-3/zmax:3, so the strongest promoting/inhibiting judgments render as mid-saturation pastels instead of full red/blue. Cross-cutting: SimilarityHeatmap uses indigo->blue->green, DriverRecipeHeatmap teal->amber->red, CIBPage RdBu/YlOrRd — three unrelated ramps with no shared definition in utils/plotly.js.
polish.fix: In CIBPage.jsx compute symmetric limits from the data (`const m = Math.max(...vals.map(Math.abs)); zmin:-m, zmax:m`) so ±2 uses the full RdBu range while staying centered at 0. Add shared `DIVERGING_SCALE` / `SEQUENTIAL_SCALE` exports to utils/plotly.js and use them in all three heatmap components for a consistent look.
polish.demo_impact: The CIB matrix — the methodological heart of the pipeline — looks washed-out and pastel on the projector, underselling the strong promoting/inhibiting relationships the analysis actually found.

polish.summary: Fractional-stat tiles are copy-pasted (StructStat in LandscapePage, Stat in ArchetypesPage) to work around MetricCard's integer-only count-up, and numbers are shown at inconsistent raw precision across pages (silhouette '0.3799' vs '0.074' vs '0.17').
polish.detail: MetricCard.jsx:16 does Math.round on the eased value, so any fractional metric must bypass it; LandscapePage.jsx:18-37 and ArchetypesPage.jsx:9-28 contain near-identical duplicate tile components. Silhouette values from the payload are rendered raw with 2-4 decimals in the structure-lenses table (LandscapePage.jsx:201-203) and ArchetypesPage 'Core silhouette' tile (0.3799), with no common formatter.
polish.fix: Extend MetricCard with a `format` prop (or a `decimals` prop that switches the count-up to fractional interpolation with toFixed), replace the two duplicated Stat/StructStat components with it, and add a tiny `fmt(v, d=2)` helper in a shared util so silhouettes and fractions render with a uniform 2-decimal precision everywhere.
polish.demo_impact: Minor visual inconsistency between the Landscape and Archetypes stat rows; mostly a maintainability and polish issue rather than a live-demo risk.

polish.summary: Presentation navigation only listens for ArrowLeft/ArrowRight — presenter clickers that emit PageDown/PageUp (the most common mode) won't advance pages.
polish.detail: handleKey (lines 28-34) matches only 'ArrowRight'/'ArrowLeft'. Many presentation remotes send PageDown/PageUp (or Space) rather than arrow keys, so if the user demos with a clicker instead of the keyboard, nothing happens. Arrow-keys-while-loading was verified safe (window-level listener, useApi aborts in-flight requests on unmount), so extending the key set is risk-free.
polish.fix: Treat `e.key === 'ArrowRight' || e.key === 'PageDown' || e.key === ' '` as next and `'ArrowLeft' || 'PageUp'` as previous in usePresentationMode.js (keep the INPUT/SELECT early-return; add 'TEXTAREA' to it while there for future-proofing).
polish.demo_impact: If the presenter grabs a clicker on Monday, the deck simply doesn't advance and they must walk back to the laptop; a two-line change removes that failure mode.

polish.summary: Because two KBs exist, the top bar's branded 'R&S Horizon 35' label is permanently replaced by a lowercase emerald select reading 'radio spectrum monitoring systems'.
polish.detail: TopBar.jsx:24 renders the select whenever kbs.length > 1 (it is 2), so the polished uppercase brand label on line 38-40 never appears; the first element in the top bar on the projector is a small form control with the all-lowercase domain string from domain_profile.json ('radio spectrum monitoring systems').
polish.fix: Always render the 'R&S Horizon 35' brand span first, and place the KB select (with a capitalize class or a short label map like {spectrum: 'Spectrum', agriculture: 'Agriculture'}) right-aligned via ml-auto — or fold it into the same demo-flag gating suggested for the accidental-switch finding.
polish.demo_impact: Cosmetic but visible in every single screen of the demo: the header reads as an unstyled dev control instead of the product brand.
